get.summary: Health index, schema enumerator, and aggregator using in-memory route metadata.
get.description:
Provides a dynamic health index endpoint with automatic runtime schema
  generation for all child health endpoints.

  No Zod, no manual schemas, and no filesystem scanning are used. Instead,
  child endpoints are executed once (in internal mode) and their returned
  JSON structures are walked recursively to infer response schemas.

  Resolution behavior:

    1) GET /v1/health
         → Returns only child endpoint names.

    2) GET /v1/health?all
         → Executes each child endpoint ONCE and returns a generated
           response-schema tree for each.

    3) GET /v1/health?complete
         → Executes all child endpoints and returns full health results.

parameters.name: all
parameters.description: Generate and return response schema metadata for all child health endpoints

parameters.name: complete
parameters.description: Execute all child health endpoints and return full results

get.summary: Basic API server health check (uptime only).
get.description:
Provides a minimal API health endpoint reporting process uptime.

  Supports dual-mode execution:
    1) Internal invocation by the /v1/health aggregator
    2) External HTTP invocation via Express

  Internal mode:
    - Handler returns a HealthResponse object directly

  External mode:
    - Handler responds via res.json(HealthResponse)

get.summary: CockroachDB health check for latency, version, and build metadata.
get.description:
Provides a CockroachDB health endpoint reporting SQL latency, build metadata,
  version information, and connectivity status.

  Supports dual invocation modes:

    1) External HTTP invocation via Express
       → Handler writes JSON directly to the Express response.

    2) Internal invocation by the /v1/health aggregator
       → Handler receives a synthetic response object and MUST return a
         HealthResponse object instead of calling res.json().

  This dual-mode behavior enables:
    • Zero-latency child health execution
    • Hierarchical route metadata traversal
    • Parent → child aggregation for /v1/health?complete

Provides runtime memory usage information for both the Node.js process
  and the underlying operating system.

  External HTTP requests receive a structured JSON health response suitable
  for health checks, diagnostics, and dashboards.

  Internal invocations (such as health aggregators) receive the same object
  directly as a return value, enabling efficient collection without HTTP
  loopback.

get.summary: Basic monitor endpoint returning an OK status.
get.description:
Provides a minimal monitoring signal indicating that the API process
  is running and responsive.

  External HTTP requests receive a simple JSON object suitable for
  uptime monitoring, load balancer probes, and basic diagnostics.

  Internal invocations (such as monitoring aggregators or self-tests)
  receive the same object directly as a return value, avoiding HTTP
  loopback overhead.

Provides a minimal network monitoring endpoint that confirms the API
  server is responsive.

  Supports dual-mode execution:

    1) External HTTP invocation
       → Responds via res.json({ ok: true }).

    2) Internal invocation (future monitoring aggregator)
       → Returns the payload object directly.

  Dual-mode support allows this endpoint to be incorporated into a
  high-speed monitoring or aggregation system without HTTP loopback.

delete.description:
Deletes a single Warframe module identified by the required mod_id
  query parameter.

  If no matching record exists, the operation still succeeds and
  returns a null data payload.

parameters.name: mod_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the module to delete

get.summary: Retrieve module list and optionally resolve a single module.
get.description:
Returns a stable response containing:

  - A lightweight list of all modules
  - A single resolved module object

  Resolution priority is:
    1. mod_id query parameters (first match wins)
    2. Exact name match if no mod_id is supplied

  If no match is found, the module field is returned as an empty
  module DTO to preserve a stable response shape.

parameters.name: mod_id
parameters.description: Module identifiers to resolve; first match wins

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Exact module name to resolve if no mod_id is supplied

put.summary: Insert or update a Warframe module.
put.description:
Performs a deterministic upsert-style operation on modules using
  the following resolution order:

    1. Update by mod_id if provided
    2. Update by name if exactly one matching record exists
    3. Insert if name resolves to zero records
    4. Reject with conflict if name resolves to more than one record

  If no mod_id or name is provided, the request is treated as an
  insert operation.

delete.description:
Deletes a single warframe identified by the required warframe_id
  query parameter.

  If no matching record exists, the operation still succeeds and
  returns a null data payload.

parameters.name: warframe_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the warframe to delete

get.summary: Retrieve warframe list and optionally resolve a single warframe.
get.description:
Returns a stable response containing:

  - A lightweight list of all warframes
  - A single resolved warframe object

  If one or more warframe_id query parameters are supplied, the first
  matching warframe is resolved and returned.

  If no matching record is found, the warframe field is returned as an
  empty warframe DTO to preserve a stable response shape.

parameters.name: warframe_id
parameters.description: Warframe identifiers to resolve; first match wins

put.description:
Performs a deterministic insert-or-update operation using the following
  resolution order:

    1. Update by warframe_id if provided (highest priority)
    2. Resolve by name:
       - class defaults to "normal" if not provided
       - 0 matches → insert
       - 1 match → update
       - 2+ matches → disambiguate by resolved class
    3. Insert if no identifier is resolvable

  If multiple records still match after class disambiguation, the request
  is rejected with a conflict error.

delete.description:
Deletes a single weapon identified by the required weapon_id
  query parameter.

  If no matching record exists, the operation still succeeds and
  returns a null data payload.

parameters.name: weapon_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the weapon to delete

get.summary: Retrieve weapon list and optionally resolve a single weapon.
get.description:
Returns a stable response containing:

  - A lightweight list of all weapons
  - A single resolved weapon object

  Resolution order is:
    1. One or more weapon_id query parameters (first match wins)
    2. Exact name match
       - If multiple records share the same name, class is used
       - class defaults to "normal" when not provided
    3. No resolvable filters → empty weapon DTO

  This endpoint is read-only and has no side effects.

parameters.name: weapon_id
parameters.description: Weapon identifiers to resolve; first match wins

parameters.name: class
parameters.description: Weapon class used when resolving by name

put.description:
Performs a deterministic insert-or-update operation using the following
  resolution order:

    1. Update by weapon_id if provided (highest priority)
    2. Resolve by name:
       - class defaults to "normal" if not provided
       - 0 name matches → insert
       - 1 name match → update
       - 2+ name matches → disambiguate by class
           - 0 narrowed → insert
           - 1 narrowed → update
           - >1 narrowed → reject with conflict
    3. Insert if no identifier is resolvable

  Update operations require at least one writable field; otherwise the
  request is rejected.
